message:
build(ffs-core): add libc dep and vendor build_test_inline_ibody helper (bd-jkpli follow-up)

Two small buildability fixes for the FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY landing:

1. crates/ffs-core/Cargo.toml: declare `libc = { workspace = true }`.
   The new get_encryption_policy_v1 path returns
   std::io::Error::from_raw_os_error(libc::ENODATA) to match the upstream
   fscrypt userspace contract, and its tests read err.to_errno() against
   libc::ENODATA. Without adding libc to ffs-core's direct deps the build
   works by coincidence through transitive deps; this pins it explicitly
   so future dependency graph changes can't silently break compilation.

2. crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs (tests): replace the call to
   ffs_xattr::build_inline_ibody with a local build_test_inline_ibody
   fixture helper. ffs_xattr's public builder evolved to enforce
   invariants (name ordering, canonical alignment) that are useful for
   production code but fight the lower-level byte-layout assertions we
   want here — the test needs to synthesize a specific inline xattr
   region at a specific layout, not a validated one. The helper builds
   the region manually: EXT4_XATTR_MAGIC header, a packed entry table
   (u8 name_len, u8 name_index, u16 value_offset, u32 value_size, 4-byte
   reserved, name bytes, 4-byte aligned), values packed from the tail,
   with overlap assertion. This is strictly test-local; the production
   ibody builder stays untouched.
